rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Characterize hub genes by regulatory potential

Source: R/modules.R
characterize_hubs.Rd

Enriches the output of identify_module_hubs with metrics that help distinguish regulatory hubs (transcription factors, signalling genes) from downstream effectors.

characterize_hubs(hub_result, modules = NULL, expr = NULL, annotations = NULL)

Arguments

hub_result

Data frame from identify_module_hubs. Must contain columns gene, module, degree, global_degree, betweenness.

modules

Optional output of detect_modules. Currently unused; reserved for future module-aware metrics.

expr

Optional expression matrix (genes as rows, samples as columns) with rownames matching gene identifiers. When provided, a cv column is appended.

annotations

Optional data frame with a gene column and any additional annotation columns (e.g., is_tf, domain, family). Left-joined onto the result.

Value

The input data frame with additional columns: bridge_fraction, bt_degree_ratio, and optionally cv and annotation columns.

Metrics

bridge_fraction

Fraction of a gene's weighted co-expression connections that reach genes in other modules. Values near 0 indicate a gene embedded within its own module; values above ~0.3 suggest a regulatory coordinator. Computed as 1 - degree / global_degree.

bt_degree_ratio

Betweenness centrality divided by within-module weighted degree. A high ratio indicates importance for information flow (path bridging) relative to raw connectivity — a signature of regulatory hubs. A low ratio indicates a well-connected but non-bridging gene, typical of housekeeping or effector roles.

cv

Coefficient of variation of expression across samples. Regulators often show higher expression variability than their targets because they drive transcriptional changes. Only computed when expr is provided.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
hubs <- identify_module_hubs(mods, net, orthologs)
hubs <- characterize_hubs(hubs, mods)

# With expression variability
hubs <- characterize_hubs(hubs, mods, expr = expr_matrix)

# With TF annotations
tf_db <- data.frame(gene = c("AT1G01010", "AT2G02020"),
                    is_tf = c(TRUE, TRUE),
                    family = c("MYB", "WRKY"))
hubs <- characterize_hubs(hubs, mods, annotations = tf_db)
} # }
